title: agrostis delicatula subsp. durieui
intro: Agrostis delicatula subsp. durieui, also known as Irish bent or durie's bent grass, is a type of grass species native to Ireland and parts of the United Kingdom. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Agrostis delicatula subsp. durieui include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential uses.
